page: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled, supported by 16.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25 percent. Text processing performance is also strong. However, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth present some limitations. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth for read-only operations is weak, though large block write bandwidth remains average. Computational workloads requiring extended instructions, software compilation, or database operations show weaker performance, as evidenced by lower Passmark database and Redis SET operation metrics. LLM inference is viable for small models but degrades significantly on medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4as_v6 offers a balanced resource profile for workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or heavy multi-threaded memory throughput. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent single-core performance without the risk of resource stealing, making it a reliable option for standard workloads. The primary tradeoff lies in the weak read-only memory bandwidth and lower multi-threaded decompression speeds, which may bottleneck memory-intensive applications.

The D4as_v6 is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, basic text processing, single-threaded compression tasks, and lightweight application hosting. It is also appropriate for running small language model inference tasks or acting as a development environment where extreme multi-core scaling and high-speed local storage are not critical requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 x86_64 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, ranking in the top 25 percent of benchmarks. However, multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are average to weak, with database operations and software compilation showing lower efficiency. Qualitatively, the server provides a balanced, cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not demand high-speed local storage or intensive multi-threaded memory access. It is best suited for static web serving, lightweight application hosting, single-threaded processing, and small-scale model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for web serving and light workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for standard workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance and dedicated compute resources for standard workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage included; relies on Premium Storage capabilities
Average multi-core performance and weak read-only memory bandwidth
Best suited for static web serving, light application hosting, and small model inference
